'''Mathew Joe Smith''' (born 23 July 1987) is an English [[rugby league]] coach and former player, who will be the head coach of [[Wigan Warriors]]' academy from 2025. He began coaching in 2023 for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in a dual role as women's head coach and men's assistant coach.

As a player he played as a {{rlp|sh}} for St Helens, [[Salford Red Devils]], Wigan Warriors, and [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]], in addition to various loans. Internationally, he represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[England Knights]].

==Background==
Smith was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside]], England.

Before converting to rugby league, Smith had a youth career with [[Premier League]] association football club [[Everton F.C.|Everton]], occasionally appearing in the reserve team.

==Club career==
===St Helens (2006–2010)===
After coming through the junior ranks at St Helens, Smith made his Super League début for the club in 2006 in a match against [[Huddersfield Giants]].{{cn|date=March 2023}}

After a loan spell in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] with [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in 2008, Smith joined [[Crusaders Rugby League|Celtic Crusaders]] on a season-long loan and played for the Welsh franchise in their first season in Super League. He later had a loan spell at [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], for whom he eventually signed on a permanent basis.

After his Salford loan, Smith was recalled by his parent club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] to play in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], which St Helens lost despite a valiant performance by Smith.

===Salford (2010–2012)===
Following his successful loan spell at the club, Smith signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] after the 2010 season and spent all of 2011 and the first half of 2012 with the club, before moving to the Wigan Warriors.{{cn|date=March 2023}}

===Wigan (2012–2016)===
Two days after making his England début against The Exiles, Smith completed his switch to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] on 6 July 2012, after a successful negotiation between the clubs to the early release from his [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] contract.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18735882|title=Matty Smith completes Salford City Reds to Wigan move|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> He made his début on 3 August 2012 against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], with Wigan winning the match 48–10.<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19091987 BBC Sports]</ref>

In 2013, Matty received the number 7 jersey formerly worn by the likes of [[Thomas Leuluai]], [[Adrian Lam]], [[Shaun Edwards]] and [[Andy Gregory]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=7725&type=news |title=Warriors Announce 2013 Squad Numbers |access-date=2012-11-19 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121212122117/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=7725&type=news |archive-date=12 December 2012 |df=dmy-all }}</ref> Smith won the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] as man of the match in the [[2013 Challenge Cup#Final|2013 Challenge Cup Final]] as Wigan defeated [[Hull F.C.]] 16–0 at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work= Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|work= Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> 

He was also part of the Wigan team that beat the [[Warrington Wolves]] 30–16 in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final|Super League Grand Final]] as Wigan earned the rare distinction of a Super League and Challenge Cup double.<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> 

He ended the year with selection in the [[Super League Dream Team]].

After goalkicker [[Pat Richards]] returned to the [[NRL]] at the end of the 2013 season, Matty Smith was given the goalkicking duties for Wigan. Smith also kicked 6 drop goals in the 2014 regular season for Wigan as well as earning a spot in the 2014 Super League Dream Team. On Friday 19 September, a night after Smith scored 21 points in the 57–4 playoff rout against the [[Huddersfield Giants]], he signed a new four-year contract with the Warriors.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=9738&type=news |title=Smith Signs New Four-Year Deal |access-date=2014-09-19 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140923115320/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=9738&type=news |archive-date=23 September 2014 |df=dmy-all }}</ref> 

He played in the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref>

He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|publisher=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Smith signed a new 2-year deal, On 11 October it was announced Matty had signed a 4-year deal with former club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] for the beginning of the 2017 season.

===St Helens (2017-2018)===
On 23 June 2017, Smith kicked a long range field goal in the final second of the match against Salford to secure Saints a 25-24 victory.  St Helens had been down by three converted tries in the latter stages of the game before staging the comeback.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40347063|title=St Helens 25-24 Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

In 2018, he made seven appearances for St Helens as they won the [[League Leaders Shield]] but fell short of a grand final appearance losing to Warrington.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45721728|title=Super League semi-final: Warrington beat St Helens 18–13 to reach Grand Final|work=BBC Sport|date=4 October 2018}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons (2019)===
In 2019, he joined [[Catalans Dragons]]. On 5 December 2019, he was released by the club after making 16 appearances. He also had a loan spell with Warrington in 2019.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/50670974|title=Matty Smith leaves Catalans Dragons by mutual consent|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

==International career==
After a stellar 2014 season Smith was selected in England's 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] held in Australia and New Zealand.<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article/40725/oloughlin-named-englands-new-captain| title=O'LOUGHLIN NAMED ENGLAND'S NEW CAPTAIN FOR THE FOUR NATIONS| publisher=englandrl.co.uk| date=5 October 2014| access-date=14 November 2015}}</ref> Smith played in all 3 of England's matches.
In 2015 Smith was selected in England's 24-man team for the end-of-year internationals<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article/47305/mcnamara-names-24-man-squad-for| title=MCNAMARA NAMES 24-MAN SQUAD FOR 2015 INTERNATIONAL SERIES| publisher=englandrl.co.uk| date=11 October 2015| access-date=14 November 2015}}</ref> but did not feature in the test against France or in the first two tests of the [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|three-match test-series]] against New Zealand. He was a late call-up in the final test-match at the Wigan Warriors's home ground, DW Stadium, in place of Wigan teammate [[George Williams (rugby league, born 1994)|George Williams]]. He was influential in the final game delivering a man-of-the-match performance.

==Coaching career==
As head coach of St Helens women, he led the team to two Challenge Cups and one League Leaders Shield.

==Personal life==
In 2015, Smith set up a business with former Salford team mate [[Stefan Ratchford]] called Future Stars, which runs coaching sessions for junior rugby league players.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilkinson|first1=Phil|title=Smith: We mean business|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/news/smith-we-mean-business-1-7881821|website=Wigan Today|access-date=3 December 2017|date=28 April 2016}}</ref>
